metadata.title:
TROUBLE


(0n|h6

GREAT M01THERN EMPLOYEES
N HILLYARD SHOPS FEAR RE-
SUIT or NEW ORDER REDUC-
ING THE HOURS OF WORK PER
DAY,

pages.raw_text:
There is promise of stirring times
at Hillyard within the next few days
and 1t looks very much as though
the Great Northern shop officials were
trying to force the hand of the rs
men who went out or struck for
longer hours two weeks ago. Notice
was posted at the shops last night
stating that until further notice
blacksmiths, boilermakers, machin-
lists and those in the tinshop would
work but eight hours day, getting
the same rate per hour. Following
the recent cut in the hours worked
by men in other departments it would
indicate a great lack of business to
keep the men employed. Employees
claim the officials want to see how
far they can go and behind 1t all are
contemplating a reduction in wages
all along the line. Heretofore the
hours worked each day have been
nine. Some Of the force at Hillyard
is working eight hours day and
only getting in Ave days a week.
